name: John Dankworth
abstract: Sir John Phillip William Dankworth  (20 September 1927 – 6 February 2010), also known as Johnny Dankworth, was an English jazz composer, saxophonist, clarinettist, and bandleader. With his wife, jazz singer Dame Cleo Laine, he was a music educator and also her music director.

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: I would love to have had Cleo Laine and John Dankworth, who are great friends of mine.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Helen Humes
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee mentions hearing Cleo Laine and clarifying she went to Ronny Scott's, not Johnny Dankworth's, implying no direct professional or personal interaction with Johnny Dankworth himself.

about_quotes.speaker: Maxine Sullivan
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWER mentions Johnny Dankworth in the context of Cleo Laine's album; Interviewee does not mention any personal or professional connection to Johnny Dankworth.

about_quotes.speaker: Mary Lou Williams
about_quotes.evidence: Yes, I played at the Palladium with Heath and all bands there, Johnny Dankworth - just all the musicians I worked with there.

about_quotes.speaker: Peter Vacher
about_quotes.evidence: Others again were Shearing obviously, Johnny Dankworth would be a very powerful contributor, and Ronnie Scott for sure.
about_quotes.doc_title: Peter Vacher interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 18, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Militello
about_quotes.evidence: we’d do just like John Dankworth and Cleo Laine, where you play the unison line with the scatted vocal.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Militello interviewed by Monk Rowe, Buffalo, New York, October 14, 2002
